# Vila Vrioni

> A protected late Ottoman manor in [Berat](https://vrioni.al), Albania's UNESCO World Heritage city. Ancestral home of the Vrioni dynasty, restored by family descendants.

- [Vila Vrioni Website](https://vrioni.al)
- [Gallery](https://vrioni.al/gallery.html)
- [History](https://vrioni.al/#history)
- [The Vision](https://vrioni.al/#vision)
- [History of Berat](https://vrioni.al/berat-history.html)
- [Invest in Berat](https://vrioni.al/invest-berat.html)
- [Enquire](https://vrioni.al/#book)

## About

[Vila Vrioni](https://vrioni.al) is a heritage property in the Mangalem quarter of Berat, Albania, officially designated as a Protected Cultural Monument under the name "Objekti i Yzedin Galip Pashë Vrioni." The compound represents Albania's finest surviving example of late Ottoman residential architecture, featuring stone barrel vaults, hand-carved walnut coffered ceilings, an arched konak, and a limestone courtyard.

## The Vrioni Family

The [Vrioni dynasty](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vrioni_family) is one of Albania's most distinguished aristocratic families, spanning four centuries. Notable members include:

- [Omer Pasha Vrioni I](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Omer_Vrioni) (d. 1828) — Ottoman general
- [Mehmet Ali Pasha Vrioni](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mehmet_Ali_Vrioni) (1842–1895) — Vice-President of the League of Prizren
- [Iliaz Bey Vrioni](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilias_Vrioni) (1882–1932) — Prime Minister of Albania, signatory of Independence
- [Jusuf Vrioni](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jusuf_Vrioni) (1916–2001) — Translator, Ambassador to UNESCO
- Izedin Vrioni — Mayor of Berat (5 terms), commander in the [Vlora War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vlora_War) (1920)

## The Property

- Location: Mangalem quarter, [Berat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berat), Albania ([UNESCO World Heritage City](https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/569))
- Type: Late Ottoman manor compound
- Status: Protected Cultural Monument, under active restoration since 2018
- Features: Stone barrel vaults, walnut coffered ceilings, arched konak, limestone courtyard, Ottoman hamami
- Legal: [ECHR Vrioni and Others v. Albania (2009)](https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/eng?i=001-95306)
